full_prompt: Design a monolithic 316L stainless steel heave plate for a point-absorber wave energy converter entered into a collegiate marine energy scaled tank-test demonstration at a 1:20 Froude scale representing a full-scale 500 kW device, producing a tank-scale prototype of 0.5 m mean diameter. The heave plate is a single circular disk with radial stiffening ribs, attached at its center to a 25 mm stainless spar-shaft by a single 25 mm clearance bore (no welds, no bolted ribs, one solid body). Outer diameter 500 mm, disk thickness 10 mm, eight radial triangular ribs each 80 mm long x 20 mm tall x 6 mm thick extending from the hub to 80% radius on the lower face only, central hub bore 25 mm running the full 30 mm hub thickness. Bounding envelope 500 mm x 500 mm x 50 mm cylindrical. Material 316L stainless steel with Young's modulus 193 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.30, density 8000 kg/m^3, yield strength 205 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 515 MPa, endurance limit 240 MPa (rotating bending, seawater Reverse-R = -1 derated by 0.7 for marine environment to 168 MPa). Environment: seawater at 15 C, density 1025 kg/m^3, salinity 35 PSU. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (cyclic heave): sinusoidal pressure load on the bottom face peaking at +/-15 kPa at 0.5 Hz for 3.6e6 cycles over a 2-week tank campaign, representing the hydrodynamic added-mass reaction (Ca ~ 1.0) of water accelerated by the plate in heave with amplitude 0.2 m and period 2.0 s, with the hub bore fixed to the spar. Load Case 2 (slam event): an impulsive 80 kPa downward pressure on the bottom face for 50 ms corresponding to a slam-induced 3g plate-normal acceleration per IEC-TS 62600-2 survivability guidance. Load Case 3 (mooring snatch): a lateral 2.5 kN point load at the disk rim transmitted from a tangled mooring line, with hub fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 peak pressure, maximum von Mises stress must not exceed 68 MPa, derived from endurance/2.47 safety factor (168/2.47) to guarantee infinite life at the 3.6e6 cycle target; (R2) under LC2 slam, maximum von Mises stress must not exceed 137 MPa (yield/1.5 per IEC-TS 62600-2 ultimate limit state); (R3) under LC3 mooring snatch, maximum von Mises stress must not exceed 137 MPa; (R4) first three natural frequencies in submerged (added mass) configuration must stay above 2.0 Hz (4x the 0.5 Hz wave excitation); (R5) a steady-state CFD run with the plate oscillating at amplitude 0.2 m and 0.5 Hz in seawater must return a heave added-mass coefficient Ca of at least 0.9, the test-rig design target; (R6) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC2 must yield a first-mode factor of at least 2.5; (R7) total mass must not exceed 16 kg. Thermal and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry as a single solid body plus a report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 316L stainless-steel heave plate for a 1:20 Froude-scale tank-test prototype of a 500 kW point-absorber wave-energy converter (collegiate marine-energy demonstration). The disk has eight integral radial triangular stiffening ribs on the lower face only and attaches through a central hub bore to a 25 mm stainless spar-shaft; single solid body, no welds. Environment is seawater at 15 C (density 1025 kg/m^3). Mass <= 16 kg. Safety factors per IEC-TS 62600-2: ULS yield/1.5 for slam and mooring events, FLS using the marine-derated endurance limit (168 MPa) with SF 2.47 for infinite life at 3.6e6 cycles, buckling SF 2.5. First three submerged natural frequencies must stay >= 2.0 Hz (4x the 0.5 Hz wave excitation); target heave added-mass coefficient Ca >= 0.9.

Envelope: 500 mm OD x 10 mm thick disk, 8 radial ribs each 80 x 20 x 6 mm, 25 mm hub bore through a 30 mm hub (disk fits in 500 mm diameter x 50 mm tall cylinder).

Load cases.
- LC1 cyclic heave: +/-15 kPa sinusoidal pressure on the bottom face at 0.5 Hz for 3.6e6 cycles (added-mass Ca ~ 1.0 at 0.2 m amplitude, 2.0 s period).
- LC2 slam: 80 kPa downward pressure on the bottom face for 50 ms (3 g plate-normal).
- LC3 mooring snatch: 2.5 kN lateral point load at the disk rim (hub fixed).

prompt.title: 1:20 Froude-scale WEC heave plate for collegiate marine energy tank test
prompt.brief: 316L stainless heave plate with 8 radial ribs, 0.5 m OD, attached to a 25 mm spar. Survive fatigue + slam + mooring snatch while delivering Ca >= 0.9.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order tetrahedral with prism BL for CFD

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Near-isothermal seawater tank; no thermal gradient.

eval_coverage: IEC-TS 62600-2 ULS and FLS safety factors
submerged modal analysis with added mass
